response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong craftsmanship practices and some low-latency systems exposure (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby, C/C++ at National Instruments), but the resume shows no experience in SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, GPU or AI-accelerator performance work, or deep-learning workload optimization — the core requirements of this principal-level hardware-software co-design role. The experience bar (BS + 8+ years) is met, but the domain mismatch is fundamental.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, open-source contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at the hardware level

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture background
